crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_msg: ET HUNTING GENERIC SUSPICIOUS POST to Dotted Quad with Fake Browser 2
crowdsourced_ids_results.rule_raw: alert http $HOME_NET any -> $EXTERNAL_NET any (msg:"ET HUNTING GENERIC SUSPICIOUS POST to Dotted Quad with Fake Browser 2"; flow:established,to_server; http.method; content:"POST"; http.user_agent; content:"|20|Firefox/"; nocase; fast_pattern; http.host; pcre:"/^(?:\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}/"; http.header_names; content:"|0d 0a|Host|0d 0a|"; depth:8; content:!"Accept-Encoding"; content:!"Referer"; content:!"X-Requested-With"; nocase; classtype:bad-unknown; sid:2018359; rev:4; metadata:created_at 2014_04_04, former_category INFO, updated_at 2020_08_20;)

sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detects a Windows program executable started from a suspicious folder
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Florian Roth (Nextron Systems), Patrick Bareiss, Anton Kutepov, oscd.community, Nasreddine Bencherchali

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Use NTFS Short Name in Command Line
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description: Detect use of the Windows 8.3 short name. Which could be used as a method to avoid command-line detection

sigma_analysis_results.rule_title: Files With System Process Name In Unsuspected Locations
sigma_analysis_results.rule_description:
Detects the creation of an executable with a system process name in folders other than the system ones (System32, SysWOW64, etc.).
It is highly recommended to perform an initial baseline before using this rule in production.
sigma_analysis_results.rule_author: Sander Wiebing, Tim Shelton, Nasreddine Bencherchali (Nextron Systems)

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (DLL) (GUI) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

products: Dell Systems Management Tools and Documentation

attributes.magic: PE32 executable (console) Intel 80386, for MS Windows

crowdsourced_ai_results.analysis: The analyzed binary is a standard Windows system utility, specifically the 'ping' command-line tool. The functionality revolves around network diagnostics, as evidenced by the use of ICMP-related APIs (IcmpCreateFile, Icmp6CreateFile, IcmpSendEcho2Ex, Icmp6SendEcho2) and network address resolution (getaddrinfo, inet_ntoa, getnameinfo). The binary parses command-line arguments to configure ping parameters, queries the Windows Registry (SYSTEM\CurrentControlSet\Services\Tcpip\Parameters\DefaultTTL) for system-wide network settings, and outputs connection statistics using localized message resources. There are no indications of malicious behavior or unexpected code execution.
